skill.name: calendly-automation
skill.description: Automate Calendly scheduling, event management, invitee tracking, availability checks, and organization administration via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Manage Calendly events, invitees, scheduling links, and organization administration through Composio's Calendly toolkit
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 170 static findings are false positives. Backticks are markdown code formatting syntax (e.g., `CALENDLY_GET_CURRENT_USER`), not shell execution. URLs are Calendly API documentation examples, not executable code. 'Weak crypto' detections refer to the string 'md' in 'SKILL.md' file references. This is pure documentation with no executable code.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives
low_findings.description: All 170 detected patterns are documentation artifacts. Backticks (155 instances) are markdown inline code syntax for tool names like `CALENDLY_GET_CURRENT_USER`. URLs (6 instances) are Calendly API endpoint examples. 'Weak crypto' flags (5 instances) detect 'md' in 'SKILL.md' file path references. No executable code present.

content.user_title: Automate Calendly Scheduling and Event Management
content.value_statement: Eliminate manual scheduling workflows by automating Calendly operations through Composio integration. List events, manage invitees, create scheduling links, and administer organizations without leaving your development environment.
actual_capabilities: List upcoming and past Calendly events with filtering by status, date range, and invitee email
Manage event invitees by listing all attendees and retrieving detailed invitee information
Create single-use scheduling links for specific event types with custom parameters
Check available time slots for any event type within a 7-day range
Cancel scheduled Calendly events with optional cancellation reasons
Manage organization invitations and memberships including sending invites and removing users
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active Calendly OAuth authentication
User must resolve Calendly URIs using CALENDLY_GET_CURRENT_USER before most operations
Organization and group-scoped queries require admin privileges
Availability checks limited to 7-day ranges per query
Cancellation operations are irreversible and trigger notifications to invitees
Rate limited by Calendly API; batch operations require responsible pagination

use_cases.title: Executive Assistant Dashboard
use_cases.description: Build automated workflows that pull upcoming meetings, check attendee status, and generate daily agenda summaries without manually accessing Calendly.

use_cases.title: Event Coordination Automation
use_cases.description: Create custom booking flows that check real-time availability, generate scheduling links, and track invitee responses for complex multi-partner scheduling.

use_cases.title: Organization Member Management
use_cases.description: Automate team onboarding by generating organization invitations, tracking invitation status, and managing member access through programmatic workflows.

prompt_templates.title: List Upcoming Meetings
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all my active Calendly events for the next 7 days. Include the event names, start times, and the number of confirmed attendees for each event.

prompt_templates.title: Check Event Attendees
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my Calendly event titled 'Quarterly Strategy Review' and list all confirmed invitees with their email addresses and booking times.
prompt_templates.scenario: View who is attending a specific meeting

prompt_templates.title: Create Scheduling Link
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a single-use scheduling link for my 30-minute consultation event type that expires in 24 hours.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a one-time booking link for a client

prompt_templates.title: Organization Invitation Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if there are any pending invitations to our Calendly organization for new@example.com. If not, send an invitation and then list all pending invitations.

output_examples.input: List my active Calendly events for this week
Who is attending the 'Product Demo' event on February 26th?
Create a scheduling link for my 30-minute consultation
output: Expires after 1 booking. Event type: 30 Minute Consultation

best_practices: Always call CALENDLY_GET_CURRENT_USER first to retrieve your user URI and organization URI before performing any operations
Use explicit date ranges with min_start_time and max_start_time parameters to improve query performance and reduce pagination needs
Get explicit user confirmation before executing CALENDLY_CANCEL_EVENT as cancellations are irreversible and trigger attendee notifications
Paginate large result sets using count and page_token parameters, adding delays between requests to respect Calendly rate limits
Test organization-scoped operations carefully as they require admin privileges and cannot be performed by standard team members
anti_patterns: Do not use the string 'me' as a user parameter in list endpoints - always resolve the full Calendly API URI first
Avoid omitting scope parameters in CALENDLY_LIST_EVENTS - exactly one of user, organization, or group must be specified
Do not mix up UUID and URI parameters - some endpoints require full API URIs while others need bare UUIDs
Never create scheduling links without setting max_event_count to 1 for single-use links to prevent unintended multiple bookings

faq.question: What is Rube MCP and why is it required?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is Composio's Model Context Protocol server that provides access to the Calendly toolkit. You must add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server and authenticate with Calendly via OAuth before using this skill.

faq.question: Why do I get errors when using 'me' as the user parameter?
faq.answer: Calendly's API requires full API URIs (e.g., https://api.calendly.com/users/{uuid}), not the string 'me'. Call CALENDLY_GET_CURRENT_USER first to retrieve your correct URI.

faq.question: Can I undo event cancellations?
faq.answer: No, CALENDLY_CANCEL_EVENT is irreversible. The skill requires explicit confirmation before canceling events because cancellations trigger notifications to all attendees.

faq.question: How do I check availability for more than 7 days?
faq.answer: Split your query into multiple 7-day ranges. The CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPE_AVAILABLE_TIMES tool enforces a maximum 7-day window per request.

faq.question: Why do I get 403 errors when listing organization events?
faq.answer: Organization-scoped queries require admin privileges. Standard team members can only query their own user-scoped events. Check your Calendly permissions.

faq.question: What is the difference between event UUIDs and event URIs?
faq.answer: Event URIs are full API paths (https://api.calendly.com/events/uuid) used as owner parameters. UUIDs are bare identifiers (uuid) used for specific operations like getting event details or canceling events.
